   107→        })
   108→        debouncedSaveRef.current.pending.clear()
   109→        debouncedSaveRef.current.timer = null
   110→      }, 500)
   111→    }
   112→  }, [saveVoiceSetting])
   113→
   114→  // Wrapper for setVoiceOutputEnabled that saves setting
   115→  const setVoiceOutputEnabled = useCallback((enabled: boolean) => {
   116→    setVoiceOutputEnabledState(enabled)
   117→    saveVoiceSetting('voiceOutputEnabled', enabled)
   118→  }, [saveVoiceSetting])
   119→
   120→  // Update volume on current audio
   121→  const setVolume = useCallback((v: number) => {
   122→    const clamped = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, v))
   123→    setVolumeState(clamped)
   124→    if (audioRef.current) {
   125→      audioRef.current.volume = clamped
   126→    }
   127→    debouncedSaveVoiceSetting('voiceVolume', clamped)
   128→  }, [debouncedSaveVoiceSetting])
   129→
   130→  // Update speed and notify backend
   131→  const setSpeed = useCallback((s: number) => {
   132→    const clamped = Math.max(0.5, Math.min(2.0, s))
   133→    setSpeedState(clamped)
   134→    window.electronAPI?.voiceApplySettings?.({ ttsSpeed: clamped })
   135→    debouncedSaveVoiceSetting('voiceSpeed', clamped)
   136→  }, [debouncedSaveVoiceSetting])
   137→
   138→  // Update skipOnNew and save
   139→  const setSkipOnNew = useCallback((skip: boolean) => {
   140→    setSkipOnNewState(skip)
   141→    saveVoiceSetting('voiceSkipOnNew', skip)
   142→  }, [saveVoiceSetting])
   143→
   144→  // Set per-project voice override
   145→  const setProjectVoice = useCallback((settings: ProjectVoiceSettings | null) => {
   146→    projectVoiceRef.current = settings
   147→    if (settings?.ttsVoice && settings?.ttsEngine) {
   148→      window.electronAPI?.voiceApplySettings?.({
   149→        ttsVoice: settings.ttsVoice,
   150→        ttsEngine: settings.ttsEngine
   151→      })
   152→    } else if (globalVoiceRef.current) {
   153→      window.electronAPI?.voiceApplySettings?.({
   154→        ttsVoice: globalVoiceRef.current.voice,
   155→        ttsEngine: globalVoiceRef.current.engine as 'piper' | 'xtts'
   156→      })
   157→    }
   158→  }, [])
   159→
   160→  // Process the speak queue
   161→  const processQueue = useCallback(async () => {
   162→    if (isProcessingRef.current || speakQueueRef.current.length === 0) return
   163→
   164→    isProcessingRef.current = true
   165→    setIsSpeaking(true)
   166→    const myGeneration = processingGenerationRef.current
   167→
   168→    while (speakQueueRef.current.length > 0) {
   169→      if (processingGenerationRef.current !== myGeneration) {
   170→        return
   171→      }
   172→
   173→      const text = speakQueueRef.current.shift()
   174→      if (!text) continue
   175→
   176→      try {
   177→        if (!window.electronAPI?.voiceSpeak) {
   178→          continue
   179→        }
   180→        const result = await window.electronAPI?.voiceSpeak?.(text)
   181→
   182→        if (processingGenerationRef.current !== myGeneration) {
   183→          return
   184→        }
   185→
   186→        if (result?.success && result.audioData) {
   187→          const base64Audio = result.audioData
   188→          await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
   189→            audioResolveRef.current = resolve
   190→
   191→            const audioData = Uint8Array.from(atob(base64Audio), c => c.charCodeAt(0))
   192→            const blob = new Blob([audioData], { type: 'audio/wav' })
   193→            const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
   194→            const audio = new Audio(url)
   195→            audio.volume = volumeRef.current
   196→            audioRef.current = audio
   197→            audioUrlRef.current = url
   198→
   199→            const cleanup = () => {
   200→              URL.revokeObjectURL(url)
   201→              if (audioRef.current === audio) {
   202→                audioRef.current = null
   203→              }
   204→              if (audioUrlRef.current === url) {
   205→                audioUrlRef.current = null
   206→              }
   207→              audioResolveRef.current = null
   208→              resolve()
   209→            }
   210→
   211→            audio.onended = cleanup
   212→            audio.onerror = (e) => {
   213→              console.error('Audio playback error:', e)
   214→              cleanup()
   215→            }
   216→
   217→            audio.play().catch(e => {
   218→              console.error('Failed to play audio:', e)
   219→              cleanup()
   220→            })
   221→          })
   222→        } else if (result?.error) {
   223→          console.error('TTS error:', result.error)
   224→        }
   225→      } catch (e) {
   226→        console.error('TTS error:', e)
   227→      }
   228→    }
   229→
   230→    if (processingGenerationRef.current === myGeneration) {
   231→      isProcessingRef.current = false
   232→      setIsSpeaking(false)
   233→    }
   234→  }, [])
   235→
   236→  const speakText = useCallback((text: string) => {
   237→    if (!voiceOutputEnabledRef.current) return
   238→
   239→    const cleanText = text
   240→      .replace(/\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]/g, '')
   241→      .replace(/[\r\n]+/g, ' ')
   242→      .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
   243→      .trim()
   244→
   245→    console.log('[TTS Debug] speakText called:', {
   246→      originalLength: text.length,
   247→      cleanedLength: cleanText.length,
   248→      original: text.substring(0, 100),
   249→      cleaned: cleanText.substring(0, 100)
   250→    })
   251→
   252→    if (cleanText.length < 3) return
   253→
   254→    if (skipOnNewRef.current) {
   255→      speakQueueRef.current = []
   256→      processingGenerationRef.current++
   257→      if (audioResolveRef.current) {
   258→        audioResolveRef.current()
   259→        audioResolveRef.current = null
   260→      }
   261→      if (audioRef.current) {
   262→        audioRef.current.pause()
   263→        audioRef.current = null
   264→      }
   265→      isProcessingRef.current = false
   266→    }
   267→
   268→    speakQueueRef.current.push(cleanText)
   269→    processQueue()
   270→  }, [processQueue])
   271→
   272→  const stopSpeaking = useCallback(() => {
   273→    speakQueueRef.current = []
   274→    processingGenerationRef.current++
   275→    if (audioResolveRef.current) {
   276→      audioResolveRef.current()
   277→      audioResolveRef.current = null
   278→    }
   279→    if (audioRef.current) {
   280→      audioRef.current.pause()
   281→      audioRef.current = null
   282→    }
   283→    window.electronAPI?.voiceStopSpeaking?.()
   284→    setIsSpeaking(false)
   285→    isProcessingRef.current = false
   286→  }, [])
   287→
   288→  // Stop speaking when disabled
   289→  useEffect(() => {
   290→    if (!voiceOutputEnabled) {
   291→      stopSpeaking()
   292→    }
   293→  }, [voiceOutputEnabled, stopSpeaking])
   294→
   295→  return {
   296→    voiceOutputEnabled,
   297→    isSpeaking,
   298→    volume,
   299→    speed,
   300→    skipOnNew,
   301→    setVoiceOutputEnabled,
   302→    setVolume,
   303→    setSpeed,
   304→    setSkipOnNew,
   305→    setProjectVoice,
   306→    speakText,
   307→    stopSpeaking,
   308→    refs,
   309→    setVoiceOutputEnabledState,
   310→    setVolumeState,
   311→    setSpeedState,
   312→    setSkipOnNewState
   313→  }
   314→}
   315→

     1→// Piper TTS - speech synthesis with Piper
     2→
     3→import { app } from 'electron'
     4→import * as fs from 'fs'
     5→import * as path from 'path'
     6→import { spawn, ChildProcess } from 'child_process'
     7→
     8→import { isWindows } from '../platform.js'
     9→import { piperDir, piperVoicesDir, PIPER_BINARY_URLS } from './paths.js'
    10→import { downloadFile, extractArchive, ensureDir } from './download.js'
    11→import { logTTS } from './debug.js'
    12→import { getAnyVoicePath, getInstalledPiperVoices, getInstalledVoices } from './voice-discovery.js'
    13→import { PIPER_VOICES, type PiperVoiceName, type ProgressCallback, type TTSStatus } from './types.js'
    14→
    15→let speakingProcess: ChildProcess | null = null
    16→
    17→export function getPiperBinaryPath(): string | null {
    18→  const binaryName = isWindows ? 'piper.exe' : 'piper'
    19→  // Piper extracts to a 'piper' subdirectory
    20→  const binaryPath = path.join(piperDir, 'piper', binaryName)
    21→  if (fs.existsSync(binaryPath)) return binaryPath
    22→  // Also check direct path
    23→  const directPath = path.join(piperDir, binaryName)
    24→  if (fs.existsSync(directPath)) return directPath
    25→  // Also check system PATH (e.g., /usr/bin/piper from package manager)
    26→  if (!isWindows) {
    27→    const systemPaths = ['/usr/bin/piper', '/usr/local/bin/piper']
    28→    for (const sysPath of systemPaths) {
    29→      if (fs.existsSync(sysPath)) return sysPath
    30→    }
    31→  }
    32→  return null
    33→}
    34→
    35→export function isPiperInstalled(): boolean {
    36→  return getPiperBinaryPath() !== null
    37→}
    38→
    39→export async function checkTTS(currentVoice: string): Promise<TTSStatus> {
    40→  const piperInstalled = isPiperInstalled()
    41→  const voices = getInstalledPiperVoices()
    42→
    43→  return {
    44→    installed: piperInstalled && voices.length > 0,
    45→    engine: piperInstalled ? 'piper' : null,
    46→    voices,
    47→    currentVoice: voices.includes(currentVoice) ? currentVoice : voices[0] || null
    48→  }
    49→}
    50→
    51→export async function installPiper(
    52→  onProgress?: ProgressCallback
    53→): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
    54→  try {
    55→    ensureDir(piperDir)
    56→
    57→    const platform = process.platform as 'win32' | 'darwin' | 'linux'
    58→    const url = PIPER_BINARY_URLS[platform]
    59→    if (!url) {
    60→      return { success: false, error: `Unsupported platform: ${platform}` }
    61→    }
    62→
    63→    const ext = isWindows ? '.zip' : '.tar.gz'
    64→    const archivePath = path.join(piperDir, `piper${ext}`)
    65→
    66→    onProgress?.('Downloading Piper TTS...', 0)
    67→    await downloadFile(url, archivePath, (percent) => {
    68→      onProgress?.('Downloading Piper TTS...', percent)
    69→    })
    70→
    71→    onProgress?.('Extracting Piper TTS...', undefined)
    72→    await extractArchive(archivePath, piperDir)
    73→
    74→    // Cleanup archive
    75→    fs.unlinkSync(archivePath)
    76→
    77→    // Make binary executable on Unix
    78→    if (!isWindows) {
    79→      const binaryPath = getPiperBinaryPath()
    80→      if (binaryPath) {
    81→        fs.chmodSync(binaryPath, 0o755)
    82→      }
    83→    }
    84→
    85→    if (!isPiperInstalled()) {
    86→      return { success: false, error: 'Piper extraction failed' }
    87→    }
    88→
    89→    onProgress?.('Piper TTS installed successfully', 100)
    90→    return { success: true }
    91→  } catch (e: any) {
    92→    return { success: false, error: e.message }
    93→  }
    94→}
    95→
    96→export async function downloadPiperVoice(
    97→  voice: PiperVoiceName,
    98→  onProgress?: ProgressCallback
    99→): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
   100→  try {
   101→    ensureDir(piperVoicesDir)
   102→
   103→    const voiceInfo = PIPER_VOICES[voice]
   104→    const modelPath = path.join(piperVoicesDir, voiceInfo.file)
   105→    const configPath = path.join(piperVoicesDir, voiceInfo.config)
   106→
   107→    onProgress?.(`Downloading voice: ${voiceInfo.description}...`, 0)
   108→
   109→    // Download model file
   110→    await downloadFile(voiceInfo.url, modelPath, (percent) => {
   111→      onProgress?.('Downloading voice model...', Math.round(percent * 0.9))
   112→    })
   113→
   114→    // Download config file
   115→    await downloadFile(voiceInfo.configUrl, configPath, () => {
   116→      onProgress?.('Downloading voice config...', 95)
   117→    })
   118→
   119→    onProgress?.('Voice installed successfully', 100)
   120→    return { success: true }
   121→  } catch (e: any) {
   122→    return { success: false, error: e.message }
   123→  }
   124→}
   125→
   126→export async function speak(
   127→  text: string,
   128→  currentVoice: string,
   129→  ttsSpeed: number
   130→): Promise<{ success: boolean; audioData?: string; error?: string }> {
   131→  logTTS('piper speak() called', {
   132→    textLength: text.length,
   133→    text: text.substring(0, 200) + (text.length > 200 ? '...' : ''),
   134→    voice: currentVoice,
   135→    speed: ttsSpeed
   136→  })
   137→
   138→  const piperPath = getPiperBinaryPath()
   139→  if (!piperPath) {
   140→    logTTS('ERROR: Piper not installed')
   141→    return { success: false, error: 'Piper not installed' }
   142→  }
   143→
   144→  // Use getAnyVoicePath to support built-in, downloaded, and custom voices
   145→  let voicePaths = getAnyVoicePath(currentVoice)
   146→  let voiceToUse = currentVoice
   147→
   148→  if (!voicePaths) {
   149→    // Current voice not found - try to fall back to any available voice
   150→    const availableVoices = getInstalledVoices()
   151→    for (const voice of availableVoices) {
   152→      voicePaths = getAnyVoicePath(voice.key)
   153→      if (voicePaths) {
   154→        voiceToUse = voice.key
   155→        break
   156→      }
   157→    }
   158→    if (!voicePaths) {
   159→      return { success: false, error: 'No voices installed' }
   160→    }
   161→  }
   162→
   163→  try {
   164→    const tempDir = app.getPath('temp')
   165→    const outputPath = path.join(tempDir, `tts_${Date.now()}.wav`)
   166→
   167→    // Piper takes text from stdin and outputs WAV to file
   168→    // length_scale: <1 = faster, >1 = slower. Convert from our speed (>1 = faster)
   169→    const lengthScale = 1.0 / ttsSpeed
   170→    const args = [
   171→      '--model', voicePaths.model,
   172→      '--output_file', outputPath,
   173→      '--length_scale', lengthScale.toFixed(2)
   174→    ]
   175→
   176→    logTTS('Spawning Piper', {
   177→      piperPath,
   178→      args,
   179→      lengthScale,
   180→      model: voicePaths.model,
   181→      outputPath
   182→    })
   183→
   184→    return new Promise((resolve) => {
   185→      const proc = spawn(piperPath, args)
   186→      speakingProcess = proc
   187→
   188→      let stderrOutput = ''
   189→      proc.stderr?.on('data', (data) => {
   190→        stderrOutput += data.toString()
   191→      })
   192→
   193→      logTTS('Writing text to Piper stdin', { textBytes: Buffer.byteLength(text, 'utf8') })
   194→      proc.stdin.write(text)
   195→      proc.stdin.end()
   196→
   197→      proc.on('close', (code) => {
   198→        speakingProcess = null
   199→        logTTS('Piper process closed', { code, stderrOutput: stderrOutput.substring(0, 500) })
   200→
   201→        if (code === 0 && fs.existsSync(outputPath)) {
   202→          // Read file and return as base64
   203→          const audioBuffer = fs.readFileSync(outputPath)
   204→          const audioData = audioBuffer.toString('base64')
   205→          logTTS('Audio generated successfully', {
   206→            audioSize: audioBuffer.length,
   207→            base64Length: audioData.length
   208→          })
   209→          // Clean up temp file
   210→          fs.unlinkSync(outputPath)
   211→          resolve({ success: true, audioData })
   212→        } else {
   213→          logTTS('ERROR: Piper failed', { code, stderrOutput })
   214→          resolve({ success: false, error: `Piper exited with code ${code}` })
   215→        }
   216→      })
   217→
   218→      proc.on('error', (err) => {
   219→        speakingProcess = null
   220→        logTTS('ERROR: Piper spawn error', { error: err.message })
   221→        resolve({ success: false, error: err.message })
   222→      })
   223→    })
   224→  } catch (e: any) {
   225→    return { success: false, error: e.message }
   226→  }
   227→}
   228→
   229→export function stopSpeaking(): void {
   230→  if (speakingProcess) {
   231→    speakingProcess.kill()
   232→    speakingProcess = null
   233→  }
   234→}
   235→

1→import { useRef, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import { useVoice } from '../../contexts/VoiceContext.js'
     3→import { stripAnsi } from './utils.js'
     4→import {
     5→  TTS_TAG_REGEX,
     6→  CODE_PATTERN_REGEX,
     7→  MAX_TTS_BUFFER_SIZE,
     8→  MAX_SPOKEN_SET_SIZE,
     9→  MIN_SILENT_PERIOD_MS,
    10→} from './constants.js'
    11→
    12→interface UseTTSOptions {
    13→  ptyId: string
    14→  isActive: boolean
    15→}
    16→
    17→interface UseTTSReturn {
    18→  processTTSChunk: (cleanChunk: string) => void
    19→  handleUserInput: (data: string) => void
    20→  resetTTSState: () => void
    21→  prePopulateSpokenContent: (chunks: string[]) => void
    22→}
    23→
    24→/**
    25→ * Hook for handling Text-to-Speech functionality in the terminal.
    26→ * Extracts TTS tags from terminal output and speaks content when appropriate.
    27→ */
    28→export function useTTS({ ptyId, isActive }: UseTTSOptions): UseTTSReturn {
    29→  const { voiceOutputEnabled, speakText } = useVoice()
    30→
    31→  // Refs for TTS state
    32→  const voiceOutputEnabledRef = useRef(voiceOutputEnabled)
    33→  const isActiveRef = useRef(isActive)
    34→  const spokenContentRef = useRef<Set<string>>(new Set())
    35→  const silentModeRef = useRef(true)
    36→  const sessionStartTimeRef = useRef(Date.now())
    37→  const ttsBufferRef = useRef('')
    38→
    39→  // Keep refs in sync with props
    40→  useEffect(() => {
    41→    voiceOutputEnabledRef.current = voiceOutputEnabled
    42→  }, [voiceOutputEnabled])
    43→
    44→  useEffect(() => {
    45→    isActiveRef.current = isActive
    46→  }, [isActive])
    47→
    48→  // Reset TTS state for new session
    49→  const resetTTSState = useCallback(() => {
    50→    silentModeRef.current = true
    51→    spokenContentRef.current.clear()
    52→    ttsBufferRef.current = ''
    53→    sessionStartTimeRef.current = Date.now()
    54→  }, [])
    55→
    56→  // Pre-populate spoken content from buffered data (for HMR recovery)
    57→  const prePopulateSpokenContent = useCallback((chunks: string[]) => {
    58→    for (const chunk of chunks) {
    59→      const cleanChunk = stripAnsi(chunk)
    60→      const tagRegex = /(?:«tts»|<tts>)([\s\S]*?)(?:«\/tts»|<\/tts>)/g
    61→      let match
    62→      while ((match = tagRegex.exec(cleanChunk)) !== null) {
    63→        const content = match[1].trim()
    64→        if (content.length > 3) {
    65→          spokenContentRef.current.add(content)
    66→        }
    67→      }
    68→    }
    69→  }, [])
    70→
    71→  // Handle user input to exit silent mode
    72→  const handleUserInput = useCallback((data: string) => {
    73→    // Ignore terminal control sequences
    74→    if (data.startsWith('\x1b[') && (data.endsWith('R') || data === '\x1b[I' || data === '\x1b[O')) {
    75→      return
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    // Ignore Enter and arrow keys - these navigate TOS dialogs
    79→    const isEnterKey = data === '\r' || data === '\n'
    80→    const isArrowKey = data === '\x1b[A' || data === '\x1b[B' || data === '\x1b[C' || data === '\x1b[D'
    81→    const timeSinceStart = Date.now() - sessionStartTimeRef.current
    82→
    83→    if (silentModeRef.current && !isEnterKey && !isArrowKey && timeSinceStart >= MIN_SILENT_PERIOD_MS) {
    84→      silentModeRef.current = false
    85→      // Clear TTS buffer to discard partial tags from session restoration
    86→      ttsBufferRef.current = ''
    87→    }
    88→  }, [])
    89→
    90→  // Process a chunk of terminal data for TTS
    91→  const processTTSChunk = useCallback((cleanChunk: string) => {
    92→    ttsBufferRef.current += cleanChunk
    93→
    94→    // Extract all complete TTS markers from buffer
    95→    TTS_TAG_REGEX.lastIndex = 0
    96→    let match
    97→    let lastIndex = 0
    98→
    99→    while ((match = TTS_TAG_REGEX.exec(ttsBufferRef.current)) !== null) {
   100→      lastIndex = match.index + match[0].length
   101→      const content = match[1].trim()
   102→
   103→      // Skip if content looks like code
   104→      const looksLikeCode = CODE_PATTERN_REGEX.test(content)
   105→      const looksLikeProse = content.length > 5 && /^[a-zA-Z]/.test(content) && !looksLikeCode
   106→
   107→      if (looksLikeProse && !spokenContentRef.current.has(content)) {
   108→        spokenContentRef.current.add(content)
   109→        // Only speak if: voice enabled, tab active, and not in silent mode
   110→        if (voiceOutputEnabledRef.current && isActiveRef.current && !silentModeRef.current) {
   111→          speakText(content)
   112→        }
   113→      }
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    // Keep only the part after the last complete tag
   117→    if (lastIndex > 0) {
   118→      ttsBufferRef.current = ttsBufferRef.current.substring(lastIndex)
   119→    }
   120→
   121→    // If buffer has no opening marker, clear it
   122→    if (!ttsBufferRef.current.includes('«tts') && !ttsBufferRef.current.includes('<tts')) {
   123→      ttsBufferRef.current = ''
   124→    }
   125→
   126→    // Limit buffer size
   127→    if (ttsBufferRef.current.length > MAX_TTS_BUFFER_SIZE) {
   128→      ttsBufferRef.current = ttsBufferRef.current.substring(ttsBufferRef.current.length - MAX_TTS_BUFFER_SIZE)
   129→    }
   130→
   131→    // Limit spoken set size
   132→    if (spokenContentRef.current.size > MAX_SPOKEN_SET_SIZE) {
   133→      const entries = Array.from(spokenContentRef.current)
   134→      spokenContentRef.current = new Set(entries.slice(-500))
   135→    }
   136→  }, [speakText])
   137→
   138→  return {
   139→    processTTSChunk,
   140→    handleUserInput,
   141→    resetTTSState,
   142→    prePopulateSpokenContent,
   143→  }
   144→}

     1→import { useRef, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react'

     3→import { stripAnsi } from './utils.js'
     4→import {

    16→
    17→interface UseTTSReturn {

    23→
    24→/**
    25→ * Hook for handling Text-to-Speech functionality in the terminal.
    26→ * Extracts TTS tags from terminal output and speaks content when appropriate.
    27→ */
    28→export function useTTS({ ptyId, isActive }: UseTTSOptions): UseTTSReturn {
    29→  const { voiceOutputEnabled, speakText } = useVoice()
    30→
    31→  // Refs for TTS state
    32→  const voiceOutputEnabledRef = useRef(voiceOutputEnabled)
    33→  const isActiveRef = useRef(isActive)
    34→  const spokenContentRef = useRef<Set<string>>(new Set())
    35→  const silentModeRef = useRef(true)
    36→  const sessionStartTimeRef = useRef(Date.now())
    37→  const ttsBufferRef = useRef('')
    38→
    39→  // Keep refs in sync with props
    40→  useEffect(() => {
    41→    voiceOutputEnabledRef.current = voiceOutputEnabled
    42→  }, [voiceOutputEnabled])
    43→
    44→  useEffect(() => {
    45→    isActiveRef.current = isActive
    46→  }, [isActive])
    47→
    48→  // Reset TTS state for new session
    49→  const resetTTSState = useCallback(() => {
    50→    silentModeRef.current = true
    51→    spokenContentRef.current.clear()
    52→    ttsBufferRef.current = ''
    53→    sessionStartTimeRef.current = Date.now()
    54→  }, [])
    55→
    56→  // Pre-populate spoken content from buffered data (for HMR recovery)
    57→  const prePopulateSpokenContent = useCallback((chunks: string[]) => {
    58→    for (const chunk of chunks) {
    59→      const cleanChunk = stripAnsi(chunk)
    60→      const tagRegex = /(?:«tts»|<tts>)([\s\S]*?)(?:«\/tts»|<\/tts>)/g
    61→      let match
    62→      while ((match = tagRegex.exec(cleanChunk)) !== null) {
    63→        const content = match[1].trim()
    64→        if (content.length > 3) {
    65→          spokenContentRef.current.add(content)
    66→        }
    67→      }
    68→    }
    69→  }, [])
    70→
    71→  // Handle user input to exit silent mode
    72→  const handleUserInput = useCallback((data: string) => {
    73→    // Ignore terminal control sequences
    74→    if (data.startsWith('\x1b[') && (data.endsWith('R') || data === '\x1b[I' || data === '\x1b[O')) {
    75→      return
    76→    }
    77→
    78→    // Ignore Enter and arrow keys - these navigate TOS dialogs
    79→    const isEnterKey = data === '\r' || data === '\n'
    80→    const isArrowKey = data === '\x1b[A' || data === '\x1b[B' || data === '\x1b[C' || data === '\x1b[D'
    81→    const timeSinceStart = Date.now() - sessionStartTimeRef.current
    82→
    83→    if (silentModeRef.current && !isEnterKey && !isArrowKey && timeSinceStart >= MIN_SILENT_PERIOD_MS) {
    84→      silentModeRef.current = false
    85→      // Clear TTS buffer to discard partial tags from session restoration
    86→      ttsBufferRef.current = ''
    87→    }
    88→  }, [])
    89→
    90→  // Process a chunk of terminal data for TTS
    91→  const processTTSChunk = useCallback((cleanChunk: string) => {
    92→    ttsBufferRef.current += cleanChunk
    93→
    94→    // Extract all complete TTS markers from buffer
    95→    TTS_TAG_REGEX.lastIndex = 0
    96→    let match
    97→    let lastIndex = 0
    98→
    99→    while ((match = TTS_TAG_REGEX.exec(ttsBufferRef.current)) !== null) {
   100→      lastIndex = match.index + match[0].length
   101→      const content = match[1].trim()
   102→
   103→      // Skip if content looks like code
   104→      const looksLikeCode = CODE_PATTERN_REGEX.test(content)
   105→      const looksLikeProse = content.length > 5 && /^[a-zA-Z]/.test(content) && !looksLikeCode
   106→
   107→      if (looksLikeProse && !spokenContentRef.current.has(content)) {
   108→        spokenContentRef.current.add(content)
   109→        // Only speak if: voice enabled, tab active, and not in silent mode
   110→        if (voiceOutputEnabledRef.current && isActiveRef.current && !silentModeRef.current) {
   111→          speakText(content)
   112→        }
   113→      }
   114→    }
   115→
   116→    // Keep only the part after the last complete tag
   117→    if (lastIndex > 0) {
   118→      ttsBufferRef.current = ttsBufferRef.current.substring(lastIndex)
   119→    }
   120→
   121→    // If buffer has no opening marker, clear it
   122→    if (!ttsBufferRef.current.includes('«tts') && !ttsBufferRef.current.includes('<tts')) {
   123→      ttsBufferRef.current = ''
   124→    }
   125→
   126→    // Limit buffer size
   127→    if (ttsBufferRef.current.length > MAX_TTS_BUFFER_SIZE) {
   128→      ttsBufferRef.current = ttsBufferRef.current.substring(ttsBufferRef.current.length - MAX_TTS_BUFFER_SIZE)
   129→    }
   130→
   131→    // Limit spoken set size
   132→    if (spokenContentRef.current.size > MAX_SPOKEN_SET_SIZE) {
   133→      const entries = Array.from(spokenContentRef.current)
   134→      spokenContentRef.current = new Set(entries.slice(-500))
   135→    }
   136→  }, [speakText])
   137→
   138→  return {
   139→    processTTSChunk,
   140→    handleUserInput,
   141→    resetTTSState,
   142→    prePopulateSpokenContent,
   143→  }
   144→}
   145→
```

rawInput.command:
cd "/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal" && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: voice I/O bug fixes — XTTS request ordering, Piper race, TTS resource leaks

- Serialize XTTS server requests to match responses to correct callers
  (Python server has no request IDs, so FIFO was mismatching concurrent calls)
- Serialize Piper speak() calls via promise queue to prevent concurrent
  processes from clobbering the global speakingProcess reference
- Revoke blob URLs in stopSpeaking/skipOnNew to prevent memory leaks
- Remove unused 'openvoice' engine type from all type definitions

Task: @bug-voice
Spec: @stt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

rawInput.command:
cd "/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal" && gh pr create --title "fix: voice I/O bug audit — XTTS ordering, Piper race, TTS leaks" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- **XTTS server request ordering**: Serialized requests via queue since Python server has no request IDs — FIFO was mismatching responses to wrong callers on concurrent requests
- **Piper concurrent speak() race**: Added promise-based serialization to prevent concurrent Piper processes from clobbering the global `speakingProcess` reference
- **TTS blob URL leak**: Added `URL.revokeObjectURL()` calls in `stopSpeaking()` and `skipOnNew` paths where audio is manually stopped without triggering `onended`
- **Type cleanup**: Removed unused `'openvoice'` engine variant from all 4 type definition files

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify XTTS synthesis works with rapid sequential requests (no response mismatches)
- [ ] Verify Piper TTS works when speak() is called multiple times quickly
- [ ] Verify stopping TTS playback doesn't leak blob URLs (check DevTools memory)
- [ ] Verify voice settings UI still shows only piper/xtts engine options

Task: @bug-voice
Spec: @stt

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
